_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-200001: AWS Root Account Console Login (T1078.004)
scenario: AWS root account used to log in to AWS Console — should never be used for routine operations
why_tp: userIdentity.type = Root — the AWS root account
MFAUsed = No — login without MFA is especially critical
Source IP 203.0.113.99 has never been seen for this account
Confidence 98: root account logins are binary — either authorized DR/emergency or attack
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Contact account owner out-of-band (phone/Slack) to confirm authorization
Review all API calls made during this root session in CloudTrail
If unauthorized: rotate root credentials, enable MFA, check for new IAM users/keys created
Check AWS Organizations for changes (SCPs, new accounts) during this session
